definition.pre_launch: A governed service remains pre-launch while operational readiness is incomplete, even if the implementation appears feature complete.
definition.launch_ready: A governed service may be called launch-ready only when rollback, operator ownership, and current evidence are all in place for production exposure.

notes: The service must have an operational runbook reference that is current enough to trust.
A rollback path must already be written and reviewable before launch-ready is granted.
A named operator must actively own the service at launch time.
The evidence packet must be recent enough that the launch verdict is not stale.
Production work must have a valid pre-release-scan before launch-ready can be granted.
The linked service must declare a governance kernel path and profile.
No unresolved blocking risk may remain when the launch-ready verdict is issued.
The service must have a passing DTP validator receipt that matches the current trace head before launch-ready is granted.

anti_patterns: Calling a service launch-ready because the UI looks finished while operational ownership is still unclear.
Calling a service launch-ready without a rollback plan.
Using launch-ready as a substitute for market proof.
Calling a service launch-ready when the current DTP validator receipt does not match the active trace head.
